Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}

*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 西山塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後円部、右に前方部） |所属 = [[大和古墳群]]（萱生支群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]萱生町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 13.00 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 55.22 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長114[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （一説）[[手白香皇女]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片、（伝）[[勾玉]]・[[管玉]]・鈴・[[土器]]・人造石 |史跡 = なし |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |ラベル = 西山塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北 }} '''西山塚古墳'''（にしやまづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]萱生町（かようちょう）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大和古墳群]]（萱生支群）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 [[6世紀]]前半（[[古墳時代]]後期前半）の築造と推定され、第26代[[継体天皇]][[皇后]]の[[手白香皇女]]の真陵に比定する説で知られる。 2024年に敷地内にあった古民家を利用した宿泊施設「cofunia」が開業している{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}}。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁部、萱生町集落西端の傾斜面地に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{R|説明板}}。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていないが、『山辺郡誌』によれば[[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年）の墳頂部での開墾の際に遺物出土のことがあったという{{R|説明板}}{{Sfn|西山塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北方向に向ける{{R|説明板}}。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成{{R|説明板}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が検出されている{{Sfn|西山塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされ、現在もその名残は周囲4ヶ所の溜池として見られるほか、周濠外側には外堤の遺構も認められている{{R|説明板}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、前述の明治の開墾の際には、石棺のほか勾玉・管玉・鈴・土器・人造石が出土したと伝わる（現在は所在不明）{{R|説明板}}{{Sfn|西山塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半の[[6世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|西山塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}。大多数が前期古墳である大和古墳群では例外的な後期古墳になる{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、第26代[[継体天皇]][[皇后]]の[[手白香皇女]]の真陵に比定する説が有力視される（現陵は[[天理市]]中山町の[[西殿塚古墳]]）{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}。 == 墳丘 == [[ファイル:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪片・須恵器片}}{{small|なら歴史芸術文化村企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：114[[メートル]] * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：65メートル ** 高さ：13メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：70メートル ** 高さ：8メートル 大和古墳群では西面の古墳が多数を占めており、北面の古墳は本古墳のほかに二ノ瀬池古墳のみと例外的になる{{Sfn|西山塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 被葬者 ==
'''『[[日本書紀]]』に基づく関係系図'''在位の記載は『日本人名大辞典』（講談社）に基づく。 {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||01| 01={{sup|24}} [[仁賢天皇]]
{{small|(在位:5c末)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||)|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|y|02|03| 01={{sup|26}} [[継体天皇]]
{{small|(在位:507?-531?)}}|02=[[手白香皇女|{{color|#FC4E6B|手白香皇女}}]]|03={{sup|25}} [[武烈天皇]]
{{small|(在位:5c末-6c初)}}}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|| 01={{sup|29}} [[欽明天皇]]
{{small|(在位:539?-571?)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
[[File:821027 Imashiroduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[今城塚古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]）}}{{Small|6世紀前半頃の築造で、一説に[[継体天皇]]（在位：[[507年]]?-[[531年]]?）の真陵。{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 西山塚古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第26代[[継体天皇]][[皇后]]の'''[[手白香皇女]]'''（たしらかのひめみこ、手白髪郎女/手白髪命）に比定する説が知られる[[吉村武彦]] 『シリーズ日本古代史2 ヤマト王権（岩波新書1272）』岩波書店、2010年、pp. 116-118。[[白石太一郎]] 『古墳からみた倭国の形成と展開（日本歴史 私の最新講義）』 敬文社、2013年、pp. 264-268。{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}{{R|説明板}}。手白香皇女の陵について、『[[古事記]]』・『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はないが、『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠墓の「衾田墓」として記載され、[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町、守戸は無く山辺道勾岡上陵（[[崇神天皇]]陵）の陵戸が兼守するとする{{Sfn|衾田陵（国史）}}。 [[中世]]には手白香皇女陵の所在地に関する所伝は喪失し、[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）に[[教部省]]によって[[西殿塚古墳]]（[[天理市]]中山町）が陵所と定められたのち、これを踏襲して現在も[[宮内庁]]では西殿塚古墳が手白香皇女陵に治定される{{Sfn|衾田陵（国史）}}。しかし、考古学的には西殿塚古墳の築造年代は[[3世紀]]後半頃と推定され、手白香皇女の想定年代（[[6世紀]]前半頃）とは大きなズレが存在する{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。これに対して西山塚古墳を手白香皇女の陵に比定する説は、大和国山辺郡一帯において前期古墳が大多数を占めるなかで、西山塚古墳のみが古墳時代後期の大型前方後円墳であること{{R|白石2013}}、西山塚古墳から出土する[[埴輪]]が真の[[継体天皇|継体陵]]とされる[[今城塚古墳]]と同じ[[高槻市]]の[[新池埴輪製作遺跡|新池埴輪窯]]から供給されていることが根拠とされている「継体王朝」大巧社 2000‐11‐15 18頁。 なお手白香皇女の陵所については、父の[[仁賢天皇]]（第24代）の陵所である[[河内国|河内]]の[[古市古墳群]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]・[[藤井寺市]]）とも、夫の継体天皇の陵所と見られる[[摂津国|摂津]]の[[今城塚古墳]]（大阪府[[高槻市]]；宮内庁治定は大阪府[[茨木市]]の[[太田茶臼山古墳]]）とも異なる所在である点が注目される{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。『日本書紀』の継体天皇即位に関する伝承によれば、[[武烈天皇]]（第25代）に後継者がいなかったため、[[応神天皇]]五世孫の男大迹王（のちの継体天皇）が[[越前国|越前]]から呼ばれて即位したという{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。しかし、即位後も大和へ遷都するまでにはさらに10数年を要しており、旧来勢力からは根強い抵抗が存在したと想定される{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。このことから、継体天皇が手白香皇女を娶ったのは、旧来王統への「入り婿」の形を取って大王位の正統性を示す意図であったと見られ、初期ヤマト王権の大王墓の墓域地として知られる大和古墳群・[[柳本古墳群]]・[[纒向古墳群]]の地に手白香皇女の陵が営まれたのも、そうした大王位の正統性を示す行為の一環{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}、または王統の始祖である[[崇神天皇]]の陵の近くということを意識してではないかともいわれる「継体王朝」大巧社 2000 18‐19頁。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2010年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=121|ref={{Harvid|天理市の文化財|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=55|ref={{Harvid|天理の古墳100|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[川副武胤]] 「手白髪皇女」|ref={{Harvid|手白髪皇女（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「衾田陵」（手白髪皇女項目内）|ref={{Harvid|衾田陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49030-1|ref={{Harvid|西山塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|西山塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=卜部行弘|year=2001|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=大和前方後円墳集成|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=4-311-30327-0|ref={{Harvid|西山塚古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} * [[手白香皇女]] * [[西殿塚古墳]] * [[今城塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:にしやまつかこふん}} [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:山辺の道]]

敬文社、2013年、pp. 264-268。{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}{{R|説明板}}。手白香皇女の陵について、『[[古事記]]』・『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はないが、『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠墓の「衾田墓」として記載され、[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町、守戸は無く山辺道勾岡上陵（[[崇神天皇]]陵）の陵戸が兼守するとする{{Sfn|衾田陵（国史）}}。 [[中世]]には手白香皇女陵の所在地に関する所伝は喪失し、[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）に[[教部省]]によって[[西殿塚古墳]]（[[天理市]]中山町）が陵所と定められたのち、これを踏襲して現在も[[宮内庁]]では西殿塚古墳が手白香皇女陵に治定される{{Sfn|衾田陵（国史）}}。しかし、考古学的には西殿塚古墳の築造年代は[[3世紀]]後半頃と推定され、手白香皇女の想定年代（[[6世紀]]前半頃）とは大きなズレが存在する{{Sfn|天理の古墳100|2015|p=55}}{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。これに対して西山塚古墳を手白香皇女の陵に比定する説は、大和国山辺郡一帯において前期古墳が大多数を占めるなかで、西山塚古墳のみが古墳時代後期の大型前方後円墳であること{{R|白石2013}}、西山塚古墳から出土する[[埴輪]]が真の[[継体天皇|継体陵]]とされる[[今城塚古墳]]と同じ[[高槻市]]の[[新池埴輪製作遺跡|新池埴輪窯]]から供給されていることが根拠とされている「継体王朝」大巧社 2000‐11‐15 18頁。 なお手白香皇女の陵所については、父の[[仁賢天皇]]（第24代）の陵所である[[河内国|河内]]の[[古市古墳群]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]・[[藤井寺市]]）とも、夫の継体天皇の陵所と見られる[[摂津国|摂津]]の[[今城塚古墳]]（大阪府[[高槻市]]；宮内庁治定は大阪府[[茨木市]]の[[太田茶臼山古墳]]）とも異なる所在である点が注目される{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。『日本書紀』の継体天皇即位に関する伝承によれば、[[武烈天皇]]（第25代）に後継者がいなかったため、[[応神天皇]]五世孫の男大迹王（のちの継体天皇）が[[越前国|越前]]から呼ばれて即位したという{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。しかし、即位後も大和へ遷都するまでにはさらに10数年を要しており、旧来勢力からは根強い抵抗が存在したと想定される{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}。このことから、継体天皇が手白香皇女を娶ったのは、旧来王統への「入り婿」の形を取って大王位の正統性を示す意図であったと見られ、初期ヤマト王権の大王墓の墓域地として知られる大和古墳群・[[柳本古墳群]]・[[纒向古墳群]]の地に手白香皇女の陵が営まれたのも、そうした大王位の正統性を示す行為の一環{{R|吉村2010|白石2013}}、または王統の始祖である[[崇神天皇]]の陵の近くということを意識してではないかともいわれる「継体王朝」大巧社 2000 18‐19頁。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2010年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=121|ref={{Harvid|天理市の文化財|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=55|ref={{Harvid|天理の古墳100|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[川副武胤]] 「手白髪皇女」|ref={{Harvid|手白髪皇女（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「衾田陵」（手白髪皇女項目内）|ref={{Harvid|衾田陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49030-1|ref={{Harvid|西山塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|西山塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=卜部行弘|year=2001|chapter=西山塚古墳|title=大和前方後円墳集成|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=4-311-30327-0|ref={{Harvid|西山塚古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} * [[手白香皇女]] * [[西殿塚古墳]] * [[今城塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:にしやまつかこふん}} [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:山辺の道]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|12=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]]
