Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Source Everybodywiki]] to [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Group]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}

* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Group]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki|qq=}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Tashiraka|qq=}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Group]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Surface features include [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (paving stones) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki]], Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki]], the same kilns associated with [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Group]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Ōyamato Kofun Group" to "Yamato Kofun Group"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun|Nishiyamazuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original location[[:en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:57, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Yamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (paving stones) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Yamato Kofun Group, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki]], Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki]], the same kilns associated with [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Yamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Group|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamanobe Road]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] }} [[every:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[every:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nishiyamatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|qq=}} in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki]], the same kilns associated with [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
