revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.570278|N|135.848672|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}}}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|1=Kayō Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998422}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (paving stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (paving stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

[[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.570278|N|135.848672|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|1=Princess Tashiraka}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}}}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|1=Q23998422|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998422}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

[[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.570278|N|135.848672|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|1=Princess Tashiraka}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}}}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|1=Q23998422|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998422}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (paving stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.570278|N|135.848672|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|1=Princess Tashiraka}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}}}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|1=Q23998422|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998422}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (paving stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Everybodywiki import successful]] (crud category cleanup) [[git:Emma-Leonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/22744539680|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.570278|N|135.848672|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|1=Princess Tashiraka}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}}}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|1=Q23998422|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998422}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (paving stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishiyamazuka Kofun]][[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:西山塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishiyamazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.570278|N|135.848672|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|1=Princess Tashiraka}}|part_of={{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}}}} {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}} is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kayō Town|ja|萱生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Town|1=Q23998422|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998422}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayō Subgroup) and remains unregistered as a historic site. This kofun was constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the early part of the late Kofun period. It is associated with a theory that identifies it as the potential burial site (真陵) of {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} (the 26th Emperor of Japan). In 2024, a lodging facility called "cofunia," utilizing a traditional house on the site, opened for public use.{{Cite web |title=「古墳のふもとの古民家」を譲り受けたcofunia 前田知里さんに聞く、何もない状況から仲間を集めて宿をつくる方法 |url=https://greenz.jp/2024/09/14/cofunia/ |website=greenz.jp |access-date=2024-12-24 |language=ja |first=greenz jp |last=グリーンズ}} == Overview == The Nishiyamazuka Kofun is located at the western edge of the Kayō Town settlement, on the slopes of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}'s eastern boundary. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound.Site information board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2010. While no excavations have been conducted to date, according to the *Yamabe District History* (山辺郡誌), artifacts were discovered during land reclamation at the summit in 1887 (Meiji 20).{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The mound faces north, with a three-tiered rear circular section and a two-tiered front rectangular section. Surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (paving stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. A surrounding moat is partially preserved as four reservoirs, with traces of an outer embankment visible beyond the moat. Artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, tubular beads, bells, pottery, and synthetic stones were reportedly unearthed during the Meiji-era excavation, but their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} The construction date is estimated to be the early 6th century, making it one of the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪片・須恵器片.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa fragments and Sue pottery}} {{small|Exhibited at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}}]] The mound dimensions are as follows:Site information board. * Total length: 114 meters * Rear circular section (3-tiered): ** Diameter: 65 meters ** Height: 13 meters * Front rectangular section (2-tiered): ** Width: 70 meters ** Height: 8 meters This kofun is unique among the predominantly west-facing Kofuns of the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster, being one of only two north-facing mounds, along with the {{ill|Ninose Pond Kofun|ja|二ノ瀬池古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninose Pond Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Identity of the interred == [[File:821027_Imashiroduka_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the interred remains uncertain, the mound is often associated with {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} (also called 手白髪郎女 or 手白髪命), the consort of Emperor Keitai.[[Yoshimura Takehiko]] (2010). *Series on Ancient Japanese History: Yamato Sovereignty.* Iwanami Shoten. ISBN 978-4004312724.[[Shiraishi Taichiro]] (2013). *The Formation and Expansion of the Yamato State Seen from Kofun.* Keibunsha. ISBN 978-4764914324. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} assigned the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} as her official tomb, which remains the designation under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Fusumada Tomb (Kokushi)}}. However, archaeological evidence suggests that Nishitonomiya Kofun dates to the late 3rd century, inconsistent with Tashiraka no Himemiko's supposed era. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka_Kofun}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西山塚古墳|version=103049823|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{everybodywiki link|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:西山塚古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%B1%B1%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Nishiyamazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11627823}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

the few late-period mounds in a region dominated by early-period kofun.{{Sfn|Nishiyamazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} {{Sfn|100 Kofun in Tenri|2015|p=55}} == Kofun dimensions == [[File:西山塚古墳出土 {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka}} {{small|Aerial view of Imashirozuka Kofun, one of Emperor Keitai's possible burial sites.}}]] While the identity of the The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} designates the burial place of Tashiraka no Himemiko as "Fusumada Tomb," situated in the Yamabe District of Yamato Province, though no precise location is detailed. The Nishiyamazuka Kofun, on the other hand, aligns more closely with her timeframe. Additional support comes from the discovery of haniwa linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja|新池埴輪窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Kilns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, the same kilns associated with {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, believed to be Emperor Keitai’s true burial site.Taikosha (2000). *Keitai Dynasty.* p. 18. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} * {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|WD=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}} *{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 7 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template (has duplicate-language pairs) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/25155405060|time triggered pipeline]]
